channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lawmaker questions the benefits of PH pursuing UN Security Council membership
item.description: In a recent statement, Rep. Rodante Marcoleta questioned the advantages for the Philippines if it seeks a seat on the United Nations Security Council (UNSC). He raised concerns about the tangible bene...

item.title: Barcelona dey consider Victor Osimhen for future striker replacement
item.description: Victor Osimhen, di Galatasaray striker and Super Eagles forward, don dey among di possible players wey Barcelona fit sign as dem dey plan for life after Robert Lewandowski. Barcelona dey look new forw...

item.title: Impact of Middle East Conflicts on Global Oil Prices and Economic Stability
item.description: The recent conflict in the Middle East has exposed vulnerabilities in the global economy, particularly in relation to hydrocarbon supply. The International Monetary Fund's director, Kristalina Georgie...

item.title: Closure of Strait of Hormuz Leaves Thousands of Ships and Seafarers Stuck Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The International Maritime Organisation (IMO) don report say the ongoing war wey dey involve US and Israel against Iran don block the crucial Strait of Hormuz. This sea route na the main gateway to th...

item.title: Highlights from Global National Broadcast on March 6, 2026
item.description: The March 6, 2026 edition of Global National, anchored by Dawna Friesen, provided a roundup of significant developments in Canada and globally. The broadcast featured discussions on Canada's foreign p...

item.title: Trump Aide Comments on India's Compliance Regarding Russian Oil Waiver Amid Iran-Israel Tensions
item.description: The news article reportedly discusses statements made by an aide of former US President Donald Trump regarding India’s response to the Russian oil waiver during the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict. The a...

item.title: Man admits to spiking drinks of two women at Derby pub
item.description: CCTV footage has revealed the moment Steven Millward, 38, allegedly spiked the drinks of two women at The Standing Order, a Wetherspoons pub in Derby, in February 2025. According to Derbyshire Police,...

item.title: VP Sara urges government action to mitigate rising fuel prices and support communities
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has called on government agencies and local government units (LGUs) to take immediate action in response to projected major fuel price hikes due to the ongoing Middle East ...

item.title: US Government Proposes Stricter AI Rules Following Dispute with Anthropic
item.description: The US government, under the Trump administration, has drafted stringent guidelines for civilian AI contracts, as reported by the Financial Times. The move comes after tensions between the Pentagon an...

item.title: Beyond Nuclear Condemns US and Israeli Military Strikes on Iran Amid Nuclear Negotiations
item.description: Beyond Nuclear, a nuclear advocacy organization, issued a strong condemnation of the recent military attacks on Iran carried out by the United States and Israel, two nuclear-armed nations. The stateme...

item.title: Poultry Industry Urges Government Support for Avian Flu Vaccinations
item.description: The South African poultry sector is calling on the government to fund mass vaccination against highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI), as it aims to build herd immunity ahead of potential outbreaks....

item.title: Lawyer Raises Concerns Over Selective Demolitions Along Thika Road
item.description: Nairobi has witnessed renewed demolitions along the busy Thika Superhighway, targeting structures and roadside businesses on road reserves. The Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has said the e...

item.title: CRDB and MCL Promote Financial Access and Opportunities for Women Entrepreneurs
item.description: Dar es Salaam hosted a significant forum on International Women’s Day, where CRDB Bank and Mwananchi Communications Limited (MCL) highlighted the importance of empowering women in business and finance...

item.title: Key Nigerian News Highlights: Peter Obi's Presidential Bid, INEC's Trust Deficit, and More
item.description: Ahahn! Today's Nigerian newspaper headlines dey burst! First of all, Peter Obi, say make we forget about the drama, e swear say e go contest for president for 2027, no matter wetin dem do am. E talk s...

item.title: President Tinubu Condoles Families, Orders Military Action After Borno Terror Attack
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don show serious sadness over the recent Boko Haram wahala wey happen for Ngoshe community inside Gwoza Local Government Area, Borno State. According to him, the attack na ruthle...

item.title: Ukraine shares expertise in intercepting Iranian-designed drones with Gulf states
item.description: Since the start of Russia's invasion in February 2022, Ukraine has been engaged in an intense form of drone warfare, developing cost-effective and versatile systems to intercept and neutralize Iranian...

item.title: Police Seek Public Assistance to Locate Missing Teenage Girls in Canberra
item.description: ACT Policing is seeking the public's assistance in locating two teenage girls, Layla Biden, 14, and Ruby Riters, 13, who are currently missing from Canberra. Layla was last seen on Wednesday, 4 March,...

item.title: DOH and health advocates urge stricter vape regulations to protect Filipino youth
item.description: Health Secretary Teodoro Herbosa has reiterated his push to raise the minimum age for buying vape and tobacco products to 25 years old and to increase taxes on nicotine products, citing the risks to y...

item.title: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia Discuss Strategies to Counter Iranian Attacks
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces of Pakistan, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, held an important meeting with Saudi Defence Minister Prince Khalid bin Salman to discuss the recent Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia...

item.title: Tanzania Intensifies Efforts to Remove Invasive Weeds from Lake Victoria
item.description: The government of Tanzania is stepping up its efforts to clear invasive aquatic weeds that have been threatening Lake Victoria, one of Africa’s largest freshwater lakes. On Friday, March 6, 2026, auth...
